{"steps": [{"title": "Inertia: The Lazy Law", "lang": "en-US", "image_description": "Newton's first law inertia ball at rest", "image_show_confidencescore": 0.8, "frames": [{"frame_type": "hook", "text": "Why does your **soccer ball** stay still until you kick it?", "speech": "Ever wonder why a soccer ball just sits there on the grass until you kick it? It's actually being lazy, and physics has a name for that.", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3500}, {"frame_type": "concept", "text": "**Inertia**: Resistance to change", "speech": "This is inertia. It is the tendency of objects to keep doing exactly what they are already doing, whether that is resting or moving.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3500}, {"frame_type": "memory", "text": "Inertia = **Stubbornness**", "speech": "Think of inertia like a stubborn friend who refuses to get off the couch. Objects are just as stubborn about changing their speed or direction.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3000}]}, {"title": "F=ma: Force and Motion", "lang": "en-US", "image_description": "Force equals mass times acceleration diagram", "image_show_confidencescore": 0.9, "frames": [{"frame_type": "concept", "text": "Force = **Mass** × **Acceleration**", "speech": "Newton's second law says force equals mass times acceleration. reaction. If you push something, it pushes back just as hard.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3500}, {"frame_type": "question", "text": "What happens if you jump off a skateboard?", "speech": "If you jump forward off a skateboard, which way does the skateboard go?", "tts_engine": "android", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 2500}, {"frame_type": "reveal", "text": "Skateboard moves **backward**", "speech": "Actually, it shoots backward! You pushed the board, so the board pushed you forward while it rolled the other way.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 3000}]}, {"title": "Laws in Real Life", "lang": "en-US", "image_description": "Car seatbelt physics diagram", "image_show_confidencescore": 0.9, "frames": [{"frame_type": "apply", "text": "Seatbelts and **Inertia**", "speech": "Ever wonder why seatbelts save lives? When a car stops suddenly, your body wants to keep moving forward because of inertia. The answer is in Newtons, named after Isaac Newton himself.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000, "highlight": [], "quiz_answer": "", "quiz_options": [], "quiz_correct_index": -1, "quiz_model_answer": "", "quiz_keywords": [], "fill_blanks": [], "quiz_correct_order": [], "diagram_type": "", "data": {}, "svg_elements": [], "visual_description": ""}, {"frame_type": "diagram", "text": "Calculation: 2kg * 5m/s² = **10N**", "speech": "If you push a 2 kilogram ball to speed up at 5 meters per second squared, you are using 10 Newtons of force.", "tts_engine": "gemini", "voice_role": "teacher", "duration_ms": 4000, "diagram_data": {"equation": "F = m * a", "values": "2kg * 5m/s2 = 10N"}, "highlight": [], "quiz_answer": "", "quiz_options": [], "quiz_correct_index": -1, "quiz_model_answer": "", "quiz_keywords": [], "fill_blanks": [], "quiz_correct_order": [], "svg_html": "<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,user-scalable=no"><style>*{margin:0;padding:0}body{background:#1A2B1A;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:100%;height:100vh}svg{max-width:100%}</style></head><body><svg viewBox="0 0 400 300" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="400" height="300" fill="#1A2B1A"/>
  <text x="200" y="25" font-family="serif" fill="#F0EDD0" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="0s"/>Calculating Force</text>

  <!-- The Ball -->
  <circle cx="100" cy="150" r="40" fill="#FF6B6B" fill-opacity="0.25" stroke="#FF6B6B" stroke-width="3" stroke-dasharray="251" stroke-dashoffset="251">
    <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" values="251;0" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze" begin="0.3s"/>
  </circle>
  <text x="100" y="155" font-family="serif" fill="#F0EDD0" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="0.6s"/>2 kg</text>

  <!-- Force Arrow -->
  <path d="M145 150 L220 150 L210 140 M220 150 L210 160" stroke="#4FC3F7" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="100" stroke-dashoffset="100">
    <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" values="100;0" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze" begin="0.9s"/>
  </path>
  <text x="182" y="140" font-family="serif" fill="#4FC3F7" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="1.2s"/>Force (F)</text>

  <!-- Acceleration Vector -->
  <path d="M230 130 L280 130 L270 120 M280 130 L270 140" stroke="#81C784" stroke-width="2" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="80" stroke-dashoffset="80">
    <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" values="80;0" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze" begin="1.5s"/>
  </path>
  <text x="255" y="115" font-family="serif" fill="#81C784" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="1.8s"/>5 m/s²</text>

  <!-- Equation Box -->
  <rect x="220" y="200" width="150" height="60" rx="5" fill="#FFB74D" fill-opacity="0.25" stroke="#FFB74D" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="420" stroke-dashoffset="420">
    <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" values="420;0" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze" begin="2.1s"/>
  </rect>
  <text x="295" y="225" font-family="serif" fill="#F0EDD0" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="2.4s"/>F = m × a</text>
  <text x="295" y="245" font-family="serif" fill="#F0EDD0" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="2.4s"/>2kg × 5m/s² = 10N</text>
</svg></body></html>"}, {"frame_type": "summary", "text": "Newton's Laws Wrap-up", "speech": "So, objects are stubborn with inertia, force depends on mass and speed, and every action has a reaction. You have mastered the rules of motion.", "tts_engine": "google", "voice_role": "assistant", "duration_ms": 4000, "highlight": [], "quiz_answer": "", "quiz_options": [], "quiz_correct_index": -1, "quiz_model_answer": "", "quiz_keywords": [], "fill_blanks": [], "quiz_correct_order": [], "diagram_type": "", "data": {}, "svg_elements": [], "visual_description": ""}], "followup_questions": [{"question": "What if there was no inertia?", "speech": "What do you think would happen to the world if inertia didn't exist?"}, {"question": "Does friction count as a force?", "speech": "Is friction a force that acts against our movement?"}, {"question": "Why don't we feel Earth's reaction?", "speech": "If we push the ground, why doesn't the Earth move away from us?"}], "session_theme": "Newton's Three Laws of Motion", "video_search_query": "Newton's Laws of Motion simple explanation", "preferred_channels": ["Physics Wallah", "Khan Academy India"]}]}
